source_title: Quantifying the Momentum Correlation between Two Light Beams by Detecting One
gate_question: Does an independently measured momentum-correlation distribution predict visibility, or is the momentum variable inferred from visibility itself?

figures.observable: visibility profiles and FWHM versus pump waist
experimentally determined transverse momentum-correlation variance versus pump waist
figures.scout_role: derived momentum-correlation width, not independent held-out record

source_text_findings: The paper states visibility depends on conditional momentum probability density P(q_i|q_s).
Visibility profiles are measured by scanning interferometric phase.
The FWHM of the visibility profile is used to numerically compute the momentum-correlation variance.
This is a strong operational record-width lane, but it is an inverse problem rather than an independent no-refit validation.
